events.shortDescription: Join us for an inspiring evening on how AI is helping people unlock new levels of creativity, productivity, and innovation.

detailPageData.title: She Sharp & HCLTech: AI Empowerment: Shaping an Inclusive Digital Future
detailPageData.subtitle: Inspiring Conversations on Inclusive AI, Innovation, and the Future of Work

location.address: University Union Building, University of OtagoDunedin, New Zealandâ

fullDescription: Join us for an inspiring evening on how AI is helping people unlock new levels of creativity, productivity, and innovation.

The event features a keynote address and a panel discussion with industry leaders who will share stories and insights on how diverse perspectives in STEM lead to fair, ethical, and inclusive AI solutions.
We will also explore the impact of AI on the future of work and highlight the skills and mindsets that will help you adapt, thrive, and lead in an AI-powered world.
Get a quick look at how the Secure Code Warrior platform works and what to expect during the workshop. :https://secure-code-warrior.wistia.com/medias/iuwtu89k00
Hear directly why secure coding training is relevant for developers and teams. :https://secure-code-warrior.wistia.com/medias/ln443qckgl

speakers.name: Rasha Y. Abu-Safieh
speakers.title: Head of Digital Strategy & Partnerships, UO
speakers.bio: Rasha Abu-Safieh is Head of Digital Strategy and Partnering at the University of Otago and an internationally recognised leader in digital strategy and implementation. A Time Magazine Next Generation Leader and Edmund Hillary Fellow, Rashaâs two decades of experience span digital transformation, AI strategy, and social innovation. With qualifications in AI for Business, Digital Transformation, and Systems Change Leadership, she has co-founded award-winning social enterprises and co-led Otagoâs Digital Strategy. Based in Dunedin, Rasha champions technology that empowers people and communities through inclusive, sustainable, human-centred innovation.

speakers.name: Graeme Riley
speakers.title: CIO of Dunedin City Council
speakers.bio: Graeme Riley is a technology and digital-transformation leader with more than 25 years experience across aviation, IT, and financial services in Australia, China, Europe, and New Zealand. He has led global and national technology programmes, modernising IT organisations and delivering complex solutions across both public and private sectors. As CIO at Dunedin City Council, Graeme is focused on building digital capability that supports communities, strengthens public services, and ensures technology and AI are developed responsibly, ethically, and inclusively. He has a strong track record in building high-performing teams, simplifying complex environments, and using technology as an enabler for people and organisations.

speakers.name: Deb Sutton
speakers.title: Labour Market Advisor â Ministry of Social Dev
speakers.bio: In her current role, Deb implements employment strategies and responds to the Southern regionâs labour market demands. She builds relationships with employers, industry groups, providers, and government agencies to create initiatives that help MSD jobseekers gain sustainable employment. With over 30 yearsâ experience in training, recruitment, business development, project management, and HR across government, tertiary, manufacturing, and mining, Debâs strengths lie in people development and building win-win connections between individuals and businesses.ââ

speakers.name: Sophie Wiltshier
speakers.title: Callaghan Innovation MTF, PGDip, BA
speakers.bio: With 14+ yearsâ experience across private and public sectors, Sophie empowers startups and women in business, tech, and science. She supports frontier ventures to export globally, win tenders, and unlock $3M+ for innovation and growth. Her strategic lens and human-centred design help scale NZ businesses, protect IP, and maximise the R&D Tax Incentive. Sophie proudly contributes to Aotearoaâs innovation ecosystem, ensuring no opportunity or funding is left behind.â

speakers.name: Kerry Ryan
speakers.title: Principal Advisor for Secondary Transitions
speakers.bio: Kerry Ryan is a Principal Advisor for Secondary Transitions in Otago and Southland, with nearly 40 years of experience in education across both local and international contexts. He has taught A Level and the International Baccalaureate, and held senior leadership roles in secondary schools, including 12 years as Head of Science. Kerry is committed to culturally responsive practice and inclusive education. He is a strong advocate for teacher professional learning and development, viewing it as a key driver of student success and system transformation. Kerry is particularly mindful of the role creatives play in tech and how diverse thinking can shape more equitable futures.â

speakers.name: Steve Gadsby
speakers.title: Public Sector Lead APME, Consulting
speakers.bio: A Generalist by nature, Steve has accumulated a raft of experiences in his 30+ years in the IT Services and Consulting industries. His career spans life as a COBOL Programmer, just about every role that IT had to offer, and compulsory stints at both an NZ Telco, & a âBig 4â. He currently oversees the Public Sector activities for the recently formed Consulting business unit for HCLTech across Asia Pacific and Middle East.Being both a strategist and a deliverer, Steve adeptly travels both paths simultaneously. His passion though lies in both Education and positive societal change. He enjoys mentoring roles and the opportunity to âgive backâ to the local communities through either swinging a chainsaw at local working beeâs or sitting in the Presiding Member (Chair) of local School Boards.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

events.title: Future-Ready: Data, AI, Digital Innovation & Inclusive Careers at Vector

events.shortDescription: Join us for an inspiring evening with Vector as we explore how technology is transforming the energy sector.

detailPageData.title: Future-Ready: Data, AI, Digital Innovation & Inclusive Careers at Vector
detailPageData.subtitle: Learn how Vector is creating a smarter, more inclusive energy sectorâand how you can get involved

location.address: Level 6, 110 Carlton Gore Road, Newmarket, Auckland 1023ââ

fullDescription: Join us for an inspiring evening with Vector as we explore how technology is transforming the energy sector.
This SheSharp event will spotlight Vectorâs journey as a tech-enabled utility, showcasing real stories of innovation in Data, AI, ADMS and digital platforms.
Weâll celebrate the voices of women in STEM, inside Vector and across the wider tech community, through a dynamic panel and a 45-minute Think-a-Thon designed to spark ideas and connection.â

Whether you're curious about inclusive career pathways, passionate about solving real-world problems, or looking to grow your network, this is your chance to engage with a future-facing company committed to diversity, innovation and impact.
Level 6, 110 Carlton Gore Road, Newmarket, Auckland 1023

speakers.name: Shona Irving
speakers.title: Head of Enterprise Data and Information Management
speakers.bio: Shona Irving heads up Enterprise Data and Information Management for Vector, where sheâs all about making data work smarter for people and teams. Sheâs big on building trusted, understood, inclusive data cultures and helping others grow in their careers. Outside the office, Shona volunteers as a KaiÄrahi for Scouting Aotearoa, supporting young people to build confidence, leadership skills, and a love for the outdoors. When sheâs not wrangling data or planning the next scout adventure, youâll likely find her hiking one of Aotearoaâs stunning trails or planning the next adventure for her whÄnau. She lives by the quote: âLeave things better than you found them.ââ

speakers.name: Evelyn Hunserger
speakers.title: Data Scientist
speakers.bio: As a Data Scientist at Vector, Evelyn Hunsberger works on initiatives that apply AI and analytics to improve customer outcomes, with a strong focus on energy hardship, affordability and network planning. Evelyn supports cross-functional collaboration, develops proof-of-concept solutions, and contributes to strategic programmes by translating complex data into insights that shape policy, pricing, and operational decisions. Outside of work Evelyn is a writer & editor for the climate newsletter Climate Club, and she loves getting into the outdoors and playing niche sports like ultimate frisbee.â

speakers.name: Karen Ip
speakers.title: Head of Engineering, VTS
speakers.bio: Karen leads a team of 50+ engineers through scale-up growth at Vector. Â With experience across Professional Services and SaaS products, she is passionate about building collaborative, empowered cultures and driving continuous improvement with strong business focus. She previously led a global team of 60+ engineers across the US, Costa Rica, and New Zealand, delivering enterprise-scale solutions generating $50M+ annual revenue. Â She has experienced the full journey from joining a start-up of 30 as a Developer and leaving as a senior leader within a Fortune 500 of 40,000+.â

speakers.name: Adriana Yong
speakers.title: Delivery Analyst
speakers.bio: Adriana Yong is a Delivery Analyst at Vector, focused on enabling smooth project delivery and supporting teams through structured planning and stakeholder alignment. After starting her career in electricity metering, she transitioned into digital delivery in Vector just over a year ago and has since worked across a variety of projects and teams. Adriana is passionate about creating spaces where her team can perform at their best and feel confident raising challenges. While her main focus is on delivery and stakeholder support, she also contributes to continuous improvement initiatives like Jira optimization.â

speakers.name: Fatemah Beydoun
speakers.title: Chief Customer Officer, Secure Code Warrior
speakers.bio: Fatemah Beydoun is a founding team member of Secure Code Warrior and has been integral to the company since 2014. As Chief Customer Officer, she is responsible for turning customers into advocates, improving Customer Success maturity, and leading global Customer Success teams. Prior to Secure Code Warrior, Fatemah held roles in security analysis, operations management, and as National Director at BAE Systems Applied Intelligence and the Australian Information Security Association (AISA), a not-for-profit working to improve cybersecurity across Government, Industry, and the Community in Australia. She holds a BSc in Computing and Information Systems from Macquarie University.

speakers.name: Suzy Clark
speakers.title: Executive GM of Security, Xero
speakers.bio: Suzy is the Executive GM of Security at Xero, responsible for improving the technical security of the business, their customers and partners. She has worked in the technology sector for 26 years, both in England and New Zealand. She has held senior technology roles at organisations in a range of different industries over the last 9 years. Outside of work Suzy enjoys playing tennis, football (aka soccer) and surfing.

speakers.name: Mehika Manocha
speakers.title: Lead Software Engineer, Xero
speakers.bio: Mehika is a Lead Engineer at Xero, where she blends technical leadership with a commitment to building a more inclusive and equitable tech landscape. As co-chair of Xeroâs Tech Diversity ERG, she champions fostering a culture of belonging. She is also an active member of Xeroâs Security Champions community, promoting stronger security practices.She extends her commitment for empowerment to the broader community by facilitating coding workshops for Code Like a Girl. Mehika holds an Honours degree in Advanced Computing, with a specialisation in AI, from The Australian National University. When not at her keyboard, she recharges by hiking, practicing yoga, or getting immersed in a good book.

speakers.name: Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Founder and Chair - She Sharp
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.â

speakers.name: Vicky Enkhsaikhan
speakers.title: women in tech advocate, educator, and GenAI expert
speakers.bio: The unique possibilities technology presents for indigenous communities is at the heart of Vicky Enkhsaikhanâs journey from a nomadic childhood in the Gobi Desert to becoming the co-founder of the Empasoft Institute of Technology in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.Vicky has witnessed and supported the use of GenAI and other technologies by local software companies to protect and further her language and culture, while advocating for women in tech and providing access to education in her home country and beyond.

speakers.name: Aileen Collins
speakers.title: Head of Digital Enablement, People & Culture
speakers.bio: Aileen heads the Digital Enablement team for People & Culture at Fonterra, shaping the technology roadmap to enhance employee experiences. She also leads the enterprise people data strategy, driving governance and data-driven decision-making. With 25 years in techâincluding IT project management and technical rolesâAileen is a champion of customer-centric solutions and the intersection of people and technology.

speakers.name: Tracey Connor

speakers.bio: Tracey leads a high-performing team of delivery engineers at Fonterra, specializing in Platform, Release, Quality, and DevOps Engineering. Her focus is on enabling fast, high-quality delivery through reusable systems and business-outcome-driven approaches.

speakers.name: Kritika Law
speakers.title: Platform Owner - Salesforce
speakers.bio: Kritika leads the Salesforce CRM platform at Fonterra, where sheâs driven its evolution for over a decade. With a background in IT engineering and a Masterâs in Information Sciences, she began her career in software testing before transitioning into implementation consulting. Her passion lies in delivering impactful solutions that transform business operations. Today, she focuses on maximizing Salesforceâs strategic value through innovation and organizational enablement.

She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

events.title: THRIVE: Your Career, Your Story
events.shortDescription: Are you a soon-to-be graduate, a recent graduate, or a professional ready for a career move? This event is designed just for you.

detailPageData.title: THRIVE: Your Career, Your Story
detailPageData.subtitle: Piki Ake: Rise up Auckland

location.address: HPE, Level 19 PWC Tower, Commercial Bay, 15 Customs Street West, Auckland Central

fullDescription: Are you a soon-to-be graduate, a recent graduate, or a professional ready for a career move? This event is designed just for you.

Join us for an empowering session focused on helping you take the next step in your career journey. Whether you're searching for your first job or exploring a career transition, this event will equip you with the tools, insights, and confidence you need.

This is more than a talk â itâs a hands-on, interactive experience designed to help you build clarity, confidence, and connections. Open to all, but especially geared towardsuniversity students,recent graduates, andprofessionals seeking new opportunities.
HPE, Level 19 PWC Tower, Commercial Bay, 15 Customs Street West, Auckland Central

speakers.name: Kathryn Sandford
speakers.bio: Kathryn Sandford is a leadership coach and personal brand strategist who challenges the outdated rules around visibility, self-promotion, and influence.She works with professionals whoâve been overlooked, underestimated, or stuck at a crossroads helping them build a personal brand that reflects who they really are, not just what theyâve done. Her signature programme, Invisible to Influential, helps women 40+ and emerging leaders stop playing small and start showing up with clarity, confidence, and presence.Kathrynâs belief is simple but powerful: Your personal brand isnât a performance â itâs your presence. Self-promotion isnât ego â itâs self-respect. And visibility starts on the inside, long before the outside world catches up.â

speakers.name: Libby Lavrova
speakers.title: IBM - Data / AI Partner Technical Specialist
speakers.bio: Libby is passionate about co-creating magical and intelligent experiences with all types of New Zealand based organisations through the IBM Ecosystem.Over her 8 year career at IBM, Libby spent four years as a Cognitive Engineer in the Telco industry and for the past two years has been a Data & AI Partner Technical Specialist.Libby dedicates her time to being essential to her business partners, listening and ideating on the problem their customers have and dreaming up together the best solution with Data & AI at the centre of it all.â

speakers.name: Kai Ping (KP) Lew
speakers.title: PBTech Head of Marketing
speakers.bio: Kai Ping (KP) Lew is passionate about how technology can make a meaningful difference in everyday life. With a big-picture mindset and a natural ability to connect with people, she tells stories that highlight the value of the right technology. Drawing from a strong foundation in process improvement and stakeholder engagement, KP ensures clear communication and aligned expectations to deliver successful outcomes.She thrives in collaborative environments that embrace innovation, iteration, and honest dialogue. KP is currently the Commercial Marketing Manager at PB Tech, where she leads a team of marketers driving commercial and vendor marketing efforts, while also serving as 2IC of the wider marketing department.â

speakers.name: Vivien Tu
speakers.title: Territory Sales Manager - Veeam Software
speakers.bio: Vivien Tu is a seasoned technology sales executive with over 20 years of experience at global tech companies including VMware, Dell, IBM, and HP. Known for her can-do attitude, customer-first approach and collaborative mindset, she has helped enterprise organisations across ANZ drive transformation through technology. Vivien is passionate about supporting the next generation of tech professionals and is excited to share her career journey, lessons learned, and practical advice on thriving in the industry.â

speakers.name: Jamini Patel
speakers.title: Distribution Manager (previous HPE graduate)
speakers.bio: Jamini began her career through the Hewlett Packard Enterprise graduate programme, gaining a comprehensive introduction to the world of tech sales. This foundational experience deepened her understanding of the tech industry and sharpened her skills in sales and partner relations. She later transitioned into her current role as a Distribution Business Manager, where she continues to expand her expertise and make meaningful contributions.In addition to her core responsibilities, Jamini completed a short-term assignment in marketing communications for New Zealand, a role that allowed her to tap into her passion for marketing and social media and express her creative spirit.Beyond her day-to-day role, Jamini plays an active part in fostering workplace culture and community. She chairs the Early Career Network, where she leads the planning of events that support professional growth and networking. She is also a dedicated member of In Her Element, HPEâs Womenâs Leadership Network, where she champions womenâs development and leads initiatives that drive diversity, inclusion, and empowerment across the organisation.â

speakers.name: Isuru Fernando
speakers.bio: Role: Advisor to World Economic Forum on AI Ethics & Human Design, Ex CTO of IBM, Ex Google Cloud AI Lead, currently NZ Digital Innovation Leader at MicrosoftIsuru works at the intersection of emerging technology and economic value to drive ambitious, sustainable growth. With an academic foundation in artificial intelligence, a strong practice in human-centred design, and experience leading multidisciplinary commercial teams, he brings empathy and insight to translating client needs and aspirations into meaningful outcomes. He is currently privileged to practise his craft at Microsoft.â

speakers.name: Caro Gao
speakers.title: Customer Success Account Manager, Microsoft
speakers.bio: Caro is a Customer Success Account Manager at Microsoft with a strong background in consulting and delivery across banking and insurance. She specialises in client relationship management, digital strategy, and technology transformation. With experience spanning banking, retail, and automotive sectors, she brings expertise in UX design, Enterprise Design Thinking, and Agile methodologies. Caro is also skilled in facilitating workshops and engaging stakeholders across all levels to drive impactful customer experiences.

speakers.name: Dalia Raphael
speakers.title: Founder and Director at Delta Insights
speakers.bio: Dalia is the Founder and Managing Director of Delta Insights - An Award-winning Microsoft Consulting Company. She is a thought leader with over 20 years of experience as a senior manager responsible for Dynamic Practices across ANZ. As a highly motivated and inspirational leader, her passion has been solving business challenges and turning them into opportunities; while thoroughly enjoying creating a positive and safe environment for staff and customers, with open and transparent communication.â

speakers.name: Irene Naidu
speakers.title: SnapperNet Business Development Manager
speakers.bio: SnapperNet Business Development Manager || Founder of TechBabesNZWith over 20 years of experience in the information technology industry, Irene is a seasoned Business Development Manager known for her outstanding sales achievements, deep industry knowledge, and strong network of connections. Her career is marked by a commitment to driving growth, fostering relationships, and making a meaningful impact within the NZ tech sector.In 2023, she was honoured with the Reseller News NZ Women in ICT Award in the Achievement category and currently serves as a judge for both the Women in ICT Awards and the NZ Channel Partner Awards.She is also the founder of TechBabesNZ, a platform and podcast focused on conversations shaping New Zealandâs technology landscape. A passionate advocate for diversity in tech, Irene actively mentors women entering the ICT sector and volunteers with South Auckland STEM, encouraging more MÄori and Pasifika youth to pursue careers in technology.Her mission is clear: to make STEM accessible to all young Kiwis and help close the digital divide across Aotearoa.â

speakers.name: Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Founder and Chair - She Sharp
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.â

speakers.name: Maria Clamor
speakers.title: Principal Sales Specialist, Spark New Zealand
speakers.bio: Maria is a driven and dedicated professional with a strong work ethic and a passion for delivering excellence. As a Principal Sales Specialist at Spark NZ, she consistently upholds high standards, whether working independently or as part of a team. Known for her self-motivation and reliability, Maria takes pride in managing her workload effectively and giving her best in every situation. Her commitment to quality and teamwork makes her a valued contributor in any environment.â

speakers.name: Matthew Hall-White
speakers.title: Financial Adviser - Life and Health Insurance
speakers.bio: Matt finds fulfilment in working closely with clients, guiding them through their financial options and empowering them to make informed decisions for a secure future. He firmly believes that sound adviceâespecially during challenging timesâoffers essential protection against both financial and emotional stress.Outside of work, Matt is an avid outdoor enthusiast who enjoys tramping, running, and exploring the mountains. He values self-care and believes in the importance of finding balance amidst lifeâs demands.â

speakers.name: Justine Hinton
speakers.bio: Justine is a seasoned Senior Account Manager with over 13 years of experience in managing client relationships and driving business growth. She is passionate about delivering tailored solutions that exceed client expectations. Working closely with cross-functional teams, Justine develops strategic plans that align with business goals and support long-term success. Her client-centric approach centers on deeply understanding client needs and offering insights that enhance operational outcomes. Known for building strong, lasting partnerships, she improves client satisfaction and drives revenue growth. Justine is keen to connect with like-minded professionals and explore new opportunities for collaboration.â

speakers.name: Prasanth Pavithran
speakers.title: Senior Business Analyst - Office of the CTO at AUT
speakers.company: Office of the CTO at AUT
speakers.bio: Prasanth is an experienced Information Technology and Management professional with over 23 years of global expertise. Originally from Lutyens' Delhi, India, he has called New Zealand home for the past 17 years where he lives with his wife, two children, and their dog.Currently, he serves as a Senior Business Analyst in the Office of the Chief Technology Officer (OCTO) at Auckland University of Technology (AUT). His career includes past roles as a Capability Manager and management positions for large organisations.Beyond his professional endeavours, he contributes to the New Zealand community through coaching and mentoring young professionals for over a decade. His community involvement extends to Community Patrols NZ. He joined SheSharp to try and have a wider impact and make a difference for future generations.In his personal time, Prasanth enjoys playing badminton and exploring creative writing. As a son, husband, father, and brother, he is committed to sharing his experience while honouring both his heritage and the place he now calls home.â

speakers.name: Tuhi Cooper
speakers.title: Programme Support Lead: Entelar Group
speakers.bio: Tuhi has 15 years of experience in the mobile sector, starting in warehousing and progressing to her current role as Programme Support Lead at Entelar. She spent 12 years at a highly recognised IT hardware company, where she built and managed a small team in the replacement and refurbish department, handled stationery procurement as a commodity specialist, and served as the sole Logistics Coordinator for New Zealand.

speakers.name: Bahareh Jalili-Moghaddam
speakers.title: Call Centre Manager, PB Tech
speakers.bio: Bahareh is a passionate and energetic Call Centre Manager at PB Tech, known for turning challenges into opportunities and creating outstanding customer experiences. With a people-first mindset and a love for fast-paced environments, she leads with positivity, purpose, and a smileâbringing energy and impact to every team sheâs part of.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

events.title: AI Hackathon Festival 2025
events.shortDescription: AI Forum is hosting the 2025 AI Hackathon across Aotearoa.

detailPageData.title: AI Hackathon Festival 2025
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by SheSharp & AUT

location.address: AUT, 55 Wellesley Street East, Auckland Central 1010

fullDescription: AI Forum is hosting the 2025 AI Hackathon across Aotearoa.
Who can join? Anyone who wants to solve real-world problems across 5 critical pathways:

With only 5 years left to achieve the 2030 SDGs and 84% of targets off-track, the AI Hackathon brings together diverse minds to create technological solutions for our most pressing global challenges. You'll learn new skills, create connections, make an impact and network with mentors in our safe space to play and promote AI for Good.

Each hackathon team will pitch their solution to a judging panel and regional winners will be announced. Our national judging panel will then select 4 finalists to pitch their idea at the 2025 Aotearoa AI Summit, 18 September in Auckland, where the audience votes for the national winner.
AUT, 55 Wellesley Street East, Auckland Central 1010

speakers.name: Alejandro Davila
speakers.title: Founder of CONICAL
speakers.bio: Alejandro Davila, Founder of CONICAL, built the Auckland studio in 2016 to push the boundaries of virtual-reality storytelling. An AUT alumnus and Young NZ Innovator of the Year finalist, he earned early acclaim for The Green Fairy, New Zealandâs first VR film, later adapted into a TVNZ animated series with support from Epic Games. Under his leadership the team has delivered immersive experiences for brands including Spark, Westfield, and Coca-Cola. Alejandro is now directing Faeborne, a story-driven action-adventure fantasy video game for PlayStation currently in production.

speakers.name: Michael Fielding
speakers.bio: Michael Fielding is a strategic leader who brings order to chaos with vision, passion, and cross-disciplinary expertise. As Chief Executive at AUT Ventures and Co-Founder of Dot Ingredients, he blends experience in finance, marketing, customer service, operations, strategy, IT, IP, and R&D to uncover opportunities others overlook. Known for zigging where others zag, Michael questions the unseen, speaks persuasively, and acts decisively with empathy.

speakers.name: Maria Mingallon
speakers.bio: Maria Mingallon is an experienced engineer with over 20 years in architecture, emergent technologies, and design, with a strong focus on digital and AI-driven solutions. A recognised leader in digital innovation, she has pioneered computational design, digital fabrication, and sustainable practices in the architecture, engineering, and construction industry. Skilled in team leadership and performance enhancement through employee engagement, Maria brings deep expertise in digital transformation to drive organisational growth and success.

speakers.name: Richard Copus
speakers.bio: Richard Copus brings extensive senior leadership experience in banking and financial services, spanning strategy, technology, operations automation, and innovation. During eight years at ANZ, he has held roles from Head of Technology to Head of Innovation and Partnerships, most recently leading the AI Centre of Excellence in New Zealand to shape AI strategy, build key partnerships, and drive AI literacy and safety. A dedicated Board member at FinTechNZ, Richard is passionate about harnessing cutting-edge technology in collaboration with fintech to deliver impactful solutions for bankers, customers, and the wider financial services sector.

speakers.bio: Alan has been working in higher education and IT for many years. He built his first web page the same year the Power Macintosh launched and started his career migrating databases from hierarchical to relational modelsâhelping him dodge Y2K chaos. Since then, heâs helped embed every major tech shift into higher education. His roles have included head of IT, manager across Student & Research Administration, consultant, (briefly) University Librarian, and now Director of Data, Technology Risk & Policy. Heâs also domain lead for the Higher Education Data Reference Model, adopted by thousands of universities worldwide. Heâs especially excited about how AI can boost his average Python skills to clean up messy data.

Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

speakers.name: Anshu Maharaj
speakers.title: Product Manager
speakers.bio: AnshuÂ is a highly experienced product manager with over 20 years of experience in managing products for Enterprise SaaS and on-premises customers. Anshu has worked in a variety of industries, including Transport, Taxation & Compliance, Payroll, Records Management, Identity Services, Border Security and Passenger Management, and GIS.With hands-on experience in DevOps and Azure hosting platforms, Anshu excels in leveraging cutting-edge technology for optimal results. As a dynamic leader and research professional, she has spearheaded numerous successful Agile and Waterfall-driven initiatives. Anshu's commitment to excellence and her proactive approach to staying updated on industry trends make her a valuable asset to any team.

speakers.name: Yvonne Weidemann
speakers.title: Business Analyst
speakers.bio: Yvonne is a versatile Business Analyst specialising in Requirements Gathering, Process Improvement, and Stakeholder Communication. With expertise in Functional Testing, Risk Assessment, and User Experience Design, she ensures software projects meet stakeholder expectations. Proficient in Agile methodologies, she facilitates scrum rituals and champions continuous improvement initiatives. Yvonne excels in documentation, change management, and stays updated on industry trends to drive innovation in software development processes.

speakers.name: Lara Rimell
speakers.title: Senior Service Designer
speakers.bio: Lara is a Senior Service Designer at MYOB, with over four years of experience in the experience design world. Despite transitioning her career from project management to design at MYOB, a craving to get amongst juicy problems and make things better for users (internal and external) follows here wherever she goes.

speakers.name: Jo-Riza Camomot
speakers.title: Software developer
speakers.bio: Jo-Riza Camomot is a dynamic software developer with over a decade of experience in the tech industry. She started her software development journey by earning a Bachelor's degree in Information Technology in the Philippines, and subsequently gained professional experience.Â Over the course of her career, Jo-Riza has utilized a different range of programming languages, such as VB, C#, JavaScript, and ColdFusion. Her passion for technology has taken her from the Philippines to Singapore, and she now continues her journey in New Zealand.Â She's advocate to encourage more women to explore and engage with the world of technology.

speakers.name: Madaleen Bothma
speakers.title: Senior product manager
speakers.company: MYOB with a knack for bringing people together
speakers.bio: Madaleen Bothma is a senior product manager at MYOB with a knack for bringing people together and making things happen! With a career spanning roles such as software developer, team leader, project manager, transformation expert, and product manager, she has a wide range of experience and has had a blast along the way.Sheâs all about the magic of teamwork and believes that when diverse perspectives and strengths come together, amazing things happen. Her proudest professional moments are when everything aligns, and our work makes a real difference to real people.

speakers.name: Kelly Yeo
speakers.title: Senior Technical & Vendor Escalation (TVE)
speakers.bio: Kelly is an experienced technical support professional with 15+ years of expertise in ERP, accounting, and hospitality software systems, having supported customers across Asia Pacific and Europe. In the current role as Senior Technical & Vendor Escalation (TVE) at MYOB, Kelly bridges the gap between customers, product teams, and software vendors to resolve complex technical issues. The role focuses on managing critical escalations, driving timely incident resolution, and ensuring effective communication regarding product defects, technical problems, and commercial concerns

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

events.title: #IAmRemarkable
events.shortDescription: #IAmRemarkable is a global movement that empowers everyone, including under represented groups, to celebrate their achievements in the workplace and beyond, while challenging the social perception around self-promotion. At the heart of our movement is a 60 minute workshop open to everyone, hosted by our very own She Sharp founder and director- Dr Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh).

detailPageData.title: #IAmRemarkable
detailPageData.subtitle: By amplifying stories and research on the transformative power of self-promotion, we want to inspire every single person to share their accomplishments.

speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.

speakers.name: Moumita Das Roy
speakers.title: Commercial Communications Manager - DuluxGroup
speakers.bio: Moumita Das Roy is a cross-industry marketer with a wealth of technology marketing experience in iconic, global organisations. Recognised as LinkedIn Top Voice and Creator to Follow, she is a prolific content creator passionately engaging in conversations about Diversity and Inclusion, Social Impact, and Volunteering. Youâll find her at the crossroads of innovation and impact, creativity and purpose. Moumitaâs multicultural work environments and travels across seven continents through work and volunteering enrich her storytelling, making her contributions relatable and versatile. Besides her day job, she helps senior corporate executives, professionals and entrepreneurs in sharing their stories confidently across social media.

speakers.name: Perrin Rowland
speakers.title: Chief Product & Experience Officer
speakers.bio: When I started my journey, I never imagined where my passion for understanding people would take me. My life has moved at different rhythms, but as I get older, I become more attuned to the ride than the destination. This realization has shaped my career journey: when we center human and environmental needs in our work, transformation follows. At academyEX and Westpac, I led teams that revolutionized customer connections. Your ability to see connections others miss is your superpower. Find where your perspective meets the worldâs needsâthatâs where your greatest contribution lies. Remember, persistence in growth, change, or even satisfying my sweet tooth, builds every success story, including yours.

speakers.name: Ireen Rahiman-Manuel
speakers.title: Founder and Director of Impactdev360
speakers.bio: Ireen Rahiman-Manuel has 20+ years of experience in design, planning, and impact measurement across New Zealand, Australia, and the Pacific. A strong advocate for maternal and child health, she is the founder/director of Impactdev360 and Rapid Builders & Renovators. She specializes in leadership, strategic planning, policy development, and social sector strengthening. She has led complex programs, including STEM initiatives. Ireen is the co-chair of Auckland Councilâs Ethnic Peoples Advisory Panel, a board member for Drowning Prevention Aotearoa and Womenâs Health Action NZ, and a spokesperson for Child Poverty Action Group Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Janet Van Jenkins
speakers.title: General Manager - Flexware
speakers.bio: The youngest of four daughters born to Vietnamese refugee parents, Janet grew up with strong female role models who showed her that women can do anything. Her career has spanned market research, sales, surgical device design, education outreach, charities, consulting, and acting - a true rollercoaster of experiences. Passionate about problem-solving, education, and the arts, she blends technical expertise with creativity to drive meaningful change towards a more equitable future. As GM of FlexWare and Programme Manager for Childrenâs University Massey, Janet is dedicated to helping people reach their full potential, whether theyâre 5 or 65.

fullDescription: The Google Educator Conference is designed for digital technology teachersâtraining them in STEM subjects and creative ways to present these in New Zealand classrooms.

The purpose of the conference is to upskill all our valuable educators such as primary, intermediate, high school by presenting ideas to engage students with new technologies so they can be better equipped for the needs of twenty-first century learning.

Teachers will be equipped with the ability to tap into the creativity and enthusiasm that many students have for technology. And theyâll be exposed to exciting ways of presenting these technologies and master new skills that can directly shape the future of computer science in their classrooms.
12 Madden Street - Grid AKL - Generator.Google Maps

speakers.name: Steve Smith
speakers.title: Google for Education, NZ Program Manager
speakers.bio: Steve has been a High School Social Studies and Geography teacher for the last 16 years in New Zealand since returning from teaching in London for 2 years. Steve worked to bring Google for Education to his school and along with a colleague trained the staff in the use of Google for education. Over 8 years, Steve passionately promoted Google Tools through workshops across New Zealand. He's Level 1 and 2 Certified Educator & Trainer, attended the 2019 Sydney Innovator Academy, and is part of the Google Earth Education Experts Group. He is now at Google's Auckland office as NZ Lead for Google for Education, aiming to empower NZ educator communities, support partners, and bring more "Google goodness" to education in NZ.

speakers.name: Alliv Samson
speakers.title: CGO and Co-Founder of Kami
speakers.bio: Alliv Samson is the CGO and Co-Founder of Kami, the worldâs #1 digital classroom application that is transforming the way students and teachers engage and collaborate with learning resources. She is responsible for Kamiâs ventures into offshore markets, their marketing efforts, team growth, and user growth.Alliv graduated with a Bachelor of Arts Majors in Political Studies and Media Studies from the University of Auckland. She has mentored university students through The University of Auckland Velocity Programme and Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship, and her experience in computer science and freelance work has helped shape Kami into one of the fastest growing EdTech companies across the globe.â

speakers.name: Dr. David Parsons
speakers.title: Research Director, academyEX
speakers.bio: David Parsons is the Research Director at academyEX and Adjunct Professor at the University of Canterbury. His experience includes academic roles at Southampton Solent University in the UK and Massey University in New Zealand, and senior professional roles in the international software industry. He holds a PhD in Information Technology and has research interests in the role of AI and digital technologies in education, agile and lean thinking, curriculum design, and contemporary software development. He has produced over 200 publications, including several books on various aspects of software development. He is a Certified Member of the Association for Learning Technologies.

speakers.name: Dr. Kara Kennedy
speakers.title: Principal - AI Literacy Institute
speakers.bio: Kara Kennedy, PhD, is a scholar, author, and educator in the areas of digital and AI literacy and science fiction. She launched the AI Literacy Institute for resources and guidance on Generative AI topics, and her AI guides have been shared and translated by educators around the world. She has presented on digital and AI literacy at conferences in the US, Australia, and NZ and published journal articles on digital and information literacy and on the gender gap in Wikipedia. Her work as a learning advisor at the University of Canterbury and Manukau Institute of Technology has given her valuable insights into peopleâs struggles with technology, and she is keen to help close the digital divide and the gender gap in STEM.

speakers.name: Dr. Kathryn MacCallum
speakers.title: Associate Professor, University of Canterbury
speakers.bio: Kathryn MacCallum is an Associate Professor of Digital Education Futures at the University of Canterbury. As Director of the Digital Education Futures Lab, she leads a community of researchers exploring digital technologies in education across all contexts, from kindergarten to tertiary education. Kathryn's research focuses on the role and impact of technology in education, particularly how emerging technologies like AI can support the development of digital literacies and how this shifts educational practices and norms. Her innovative work has influenced educational practices in New Zealand and internationally. Kathryn has published extensively and serves as editor-in-chief for several leading international journals.

speakers.name: Catherine Frost
speakers.title: Lead Writer Technology , Ministry of Education
speakers.bio: Catherine is currently Lead Writer in the Technology learning area in the Ministry of Education for the curriculum refresh Te MÄtaiaho. Teaching across all sectors, first in the 90âs in the UK and since 2004 in Aotearoa New Zealand, Catherine has almost 30 years' experience in Technology education, leadership, strategy and curriculum development; Catherine has held a variety of, advisory, consultancy and national project leadership roles, specialising in change strategy, leadership with the goal to improve the educational opportunities for all Äkonga through a âfutures-readyâ lens and quality learning design. She is passionate about curriculum and the potential of emerging technologies to transform educational possibilities.

speakers.name: Dr. Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Founder of She Sharp and Senior lecturer at AUT
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.

speakers.name: Munireh Rouget
speakers.title: Deputy Chair - TENZ Council
speakers.bio: Munireh serves as the Deputy Chair on the TENZ council and is Poutakawaenga mÅ te PÅ«take Whakawhanaungatanga - Facilitator for the Whakawhanaungatanga Principle. She earned her Masterâs in Computer Sciences from UniversitÃ© Paul Sabatier in France and began her career as a Software Engineer, crafting e-commerce websites for major international companies. In 2021, she made the exciting decision to become a teacher, aiming to enhance diversity and inclusion in Computer Sciences and Digital Technologies. Now, as the Leader of Learning for Digital Technology at Fraser High School, she strives to empower young women and support them in their journeys in STEMM, particularly in Computer Science.

speakers.name: Claire Wigley
speakers.title: Teacher at Spotswood College New Plymouth
speakers.bio: Claire is a dedicated teacher at Spotswood College in New Plymouth, focused on teaching 21st-century skills. With a background in textiles technology, she has expanded her expertise over the past 8 years to include cooking, coding, and laser cutting, covering various aspects of the Technology Curriculum. Teaching students from year 1 to year 10, Claire integrates Design Thinking into her lessons as an Inquiry Specialist. In 2023, she joined the council at TENZ and played a key role in their conference. She is passionate about inspiring young women, promoting female role models, and highlighting opportunities in STEAM fields.

speakers.name: Henry Hickman
speakers.title: PhD student at the University of Canterbury
speakers.bio: Henry is a PhD student at the University of Canterbury, supervised by Tim Bell in the Computer Science Education Research Group, a.k.a the Department of Fun Stuff. His current area of research deals with automating the NCEA level one programming standard, which aims to save teachers time, improve student outcomes, and increase the equity of programming education nationwide. He is also a stand-up comedian, hosting comedy shows in Christchurch, and a big fan of the TV show Survivor, which he talks about weekly on his YouTube channel.

speakers.name: Steven Rodkiss
speakers.title: Digital Technologies Teacher, Burnside High School
speakers.bio: Steve is a digital technologies teacher at Burnside High School in Christchurch and a member of the DTTA committee. He has more than a decade of experience and takes his passion for game development and programming into his work, having created NCEA programmes of learning that let kids make games and applications for NCEA credits. Steve is committed to sharing his passion and is a founding member and content creator for Techquity, as well as having 4.3k subscribers on his game dev focussed Youtube channel!

speakers.name: Alex Ward
speakers.title: Head of Digital Technology Kerikeri High School
speakers.bio: Alex is a Teacher, Musician, and Programming Enthusiast, leads Kerikeri High School's Digital Tech department for 4 years. Now, he's taking his talents to Rototuna Junior High School in Waikato. Alex combines code and creativity to craft generative art and music, even exploring the synergy between Digital Tech and other subjects. With a Bachelors in Music and experience as a Lecturer and Programme Administrator at Massey and Victoria University, his global performances include Singapore, Korea, China, Indonesia, Australia, and Antarctica. Alex relishes the switch to Digital Tech, making technology a canvas for creativity.â

speakers.name: Bex Rose
speakers.title: Head of Impact, Growth Culture
speakers.bio: As a seasoned education leader with over two decades of experience, Bex Rose is dedicated to empowering educators to embrace the transformative potential of artificial intelligence. With a deep understanding of the challenges and opportunities presented by AI, Bex has established herself as a passionate facilitator and mentor. In her role as Head of Impact at Growth Culture, she is also a seasoned leadership facilitator with a proven track record of guiding educational leaders to develop their skills, unlock their potential, and drive positive change. Bex is a driving force in shaping the future of education in New Zealand.

speakers.name: Jessica Brennan
speakers.title: Kami - Regional Customer Manager
speakers.bio: Jessica Brennan, Regional Customer Manager at Kami, started her career teaching special education in schools, and is now a valued Regional Customer Manager at Kami, delivering exceptional service and cultivating strong global client relationships.

speakers.name: Paul Savage
speakers.title: Kami - Senior Product Marketer
speakers.bio: Paul Savage, Senior Product Marketer at Kami, brings a unique blend of educational expertise and product innovation to the workshop. With a background as a former adult education teacher and over 20 years of global product marketing experience. His mission is to bring educators tools that break down barriers in education, ensuring equitable learning opportunities for all students. Paulâs passion for blending pedagogy with technology drives his commitment to transforming classrooms into engaging, supportive environments for students of all backgrounds.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

speakers.name: Lara Higson
speakers.title: Vice President - HCLTech
speakers.bio: Lara is a seasoned senior executive with nearly 40 years of experience in the energy, mining, and technology sectors, spanning government-owned, public, and private settings. She has operated in numerous roles at company, division, and functional levels, in both corporate and site environments, in Australia and overseas. Lara holds undergraduate and post graduate qualifications including a Bachelor of Business, Certified Practicing Accountant (CPA), Masters of Business Administration (MBA), and Graduate Diploma of Applied Finance and Investment.

speakers.name: Loren George
speakers.title: Data Product Lead - Fonterra
speakers.bio: Having started in Fonterra 17 years ago in Accounts Payable and moving my way through my career to spend the last 12 years in the data space, it has been clear to me that the demand for âdata peopleâ is only getting bigger. I currently work as the Data Product Lead for Fonterra, understanding the frameworks needed to take Fonterra on itâs Data Product Transformation. The role involves a lot of indirect management, coaching and stakeholder management, all of which I am passionate about. I believe that each person has their own value and enjoy working with people to unlock the skill that makes them shine so they can bring the most to their role.

speakers.name: Noor Syed
speakers.title: Account Tech Strategist/CTO - Health Sector NZ
speakers.bio: Noor is of Pakistani origin, grown up in Dubai and has been in NZ for the last 10 years. Noor works as a Tech Strategist/Chief Technology Officer for health in NZ @Microsoft. She drives technological innovation and transformation within the healthcare sector and supports the development and implemention of strategic technology solutions to enhance patient care, streamline operations, and improve efficiency. Her expertise in both technology and healthcare helps organisations achieve their goals and deliver better patient outcomes. She also leads transformative initiatives that modernise healthcare systems and promote digital health solutions. Noor is also the external partnerships lead for GLEAM (LGBTQIA+ chapter) at Microsoft.

speakers.name: Dr Kim de Graaf
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer in Civil Engineering - UoW
speakers.bio: Kim is a senior lecturer in Civil Engineering at the University of Waikato and a Chartered Professional Geotechnical Engineer with strong industry links. She has over 10 years of experience as a geotechnical engineer through various building and infrastructure projects. After returning to academia in 2021, Kim has focussed her research interests on the behaviour of unusual NZ soils, slope instability, landslides and the use of geotechnical waste to create 3D printable sustainable concrete. Kim is also leading a research group in the early stages of the development of an AR system to support communities in understanding their risk to natural hazards and potential to improve resilience.

speakers.name: Margaret Paiti
speakers.title: Principal Advisor Secondary Transition - MOE
speakers.bio: Margaret Paiti has a solid understanding of Äotearoa's educational system. With over twenty-five years of experience across a diverse range of roles, she's fully familiar with both the challenges and opportunities within STEM education. Committed to collaboration, Margaret's goal is to strengthen relationships among various groups, driving meaningful change. A passionate advocate for STEM, she urges young people, especially those who are underserved, to delve into these fields. Her active role with the Web Access Waikato Trust demonstrates her commitment to digital inclusion. Additionally, her contributions to the Waikato Tertiary Tech Alliance emphasise her dedication to expanding STEM opportunities.â

speakers.name: Yvonne Gill
speakers.title: Executive Director - TechWomen | NZTech
speakers.bio: Yvonne is the Executive Director of TechWomen and works with an amazing group of women across New Zealand who collectively deliver a large range of programs and events that inspire girls into technology, support the growth of women in tech roles, and help develop policy and actions for improving diversity in the tech workplace.Yvonne also leads the strategic programmes across the NZTech group, which delivers hundreds of events, research reports, and initiatives nationwide to connect, promote, and advance technology in Aotearoa New Zealand for a better tomorrow.

The evening will conclude with a networking session, giving you a chance to celebrate and connect with industry leaders and fellow professionals shaping the future of tech.

speakers.name: Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Founder and Chair - She Sharp
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.â

speakers.name: Alexia Hilbertidou
speakers.title: Founder, GirlBoss New Zealand
speakers.bio: Alexia is the CEO of GirlBoss NZ - NZ's largest organisation for young women. Founded when Alexia was just16, GirlBoss now has 17,500 members. Alexiaâs mission is to get women to the table - the boardroom tablethat is, and she believes the decisions made while young are crucial in paving the way. She has flown withNASA, received a Queenâs Young Leaders Medal for Services to the Commonwealth, is a National GeographicExplorer, Forbes 30 under 30, and the youngest person to represent New Zealand at the United Nations inNew York. She was named Pacific Young Entrepreneur of the Year in 2020 and 2022.

speakers.name: Libby Lavrova
speakers.title: Data & AI Tech Specialist
speakers.bio: Libby is passionate about co-creating magical and intelligent experiences with all types of New Zealand based organisations through the IBM Ecosystem.Over her 8 year career at IBM, Libby spent four years as a Cognitive Engineer in the Telco industry and for the past two years has been a Data & AI Partner Technical Specialist.Libby dedicates her time to being essential to her business partners, listening and ideating on the problem their customers have and dreaming up together the best solution with Data & AI at the centre of it all.

speakers.name: Frances Valintine CNZM
speakers.title: Founder & Board Director, academyEX
speakers.bio: Frances Valintine CNZM is a passionate educator and technologist dedicated to creating opportunities that connect professionals with the knowledge they need to succeed in todayâs rapidly advancing world. For over 25 years, she has focused on shifting education and business practices to develop the skills, knowledge, and mindset needed for the future of work, societal change, and planetary protection.In 2024, Frances transitioned from CEO to Founder and Board Director, concentrating on major initiatives and partnerships at academyEX, a post-graduate institute specializing in technological advancement, education, sustainability, and leadership.

speakers.name: Aravind Subramanian
speakers.title: Partner in Deloitte's Technology
speakers.bio: Aravind is a Partner in Deloitteâs Technology & Transformation practice with over 20 years of professional experience leading a wide range of data-driven CX, business and digital transformation initiatives. He leads the AI & Data capability at Deloitte and specializes in AI enablement, Trustworthy AI, Data Leadership & Culture change and Strategic Data Governance.Prior to focusing on Data & Analytics, Aravind worked across customer transformation and ecommerce initiatives and thus brings a good understanding how data & insights are managed across the enterprise from client facing functions to internal business operations.

speakers.name: Rochelle Moffitt
speakers.title: Founder & CEO, Tickled Pink
speakers.bio: Championing women in business and helping them courageously up their game and amplify their presence, Rochelle is a powerhouse in New Zealandâs social media and marketing industry.With a knack for innovative strategies and a passion for bold, lasting change, Rochelle supports individuals to elevate their visibility and achieve unparalleled success.As an award-winning marketer, she specialises in strategic personal branding, compelling story-driven marketing, and effective networking relationship management. Making her the go-to expert for those who dare to be brave and are ready to shine and succeed.
