speakers.name: Saskia van Bergenhenegouwen
speakers.title: Digital Operational Technology Manager
speakers.bio: Saskia is our Digital Operational Technology Manager. She and her team collaborate with the business to deploy innovative solutions, like AI image cognition. Joining Fonterra in 2009 as an Inventory Manager, she spent 14 years in inventory and purchasing, ending as National Procurement and Materials Manager, leading a large team and focusing on simplification and standardization. After developing Power BI solutions, she moved into her current role in Automation & Operational Technology. Saskia leverages technology to enhance processes and support people. She has two young boys, 8 and 3, who keep her grounded and remind her to have fun daily.â

speakers.name: Melody Taylor
speakers.title: General Manager of the Integrated Operation Centre
speakers.bio: Melody joined Fonterra in 2023 as the GM of the Integrated Operations Centre. She generates insights from data and enables our value chain through digital innovation, leveraging AI to unlock step changes. With a Bachelor of Commerce in Information Systems, Melody began her career in technology consulting, working across Africa, Asia, the UK, and Australia on large-scale ERP implementations. She then spent 10 years in the mining industry focusing on digital transformation. Now back in NZ, she enjoys solving tough problems and merging people with technology. Melody has a 7-year-old daughter who is curious and passionate, and she encourages her to thrive.â

speakers.name: Natasha Brownlie
speakers.title: Modern Work Specialist - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Natasha is a Modern Work Specialist at Microsoft, supporting Commercial organisations across New Zealand boost productivity, flexibility, wellbeing, and connection. Holding a Masters of Commerce and with a background in human resources, she transitioned to the IT sector in 2022 by joining Microsoft. Merging her interest in both human resources and technology has allowed Natasha to focus on elevating employee experiences in todayâs hybrid work environment and the emerging Gen AI era.

speakers.name: Helius Guimaraes
speakers.title: Chief Data Officer & AI Officer - Fonterra
speakers.bio: Helius started his career in a start-up driving digital transformation. After graduating as a geologist, he implemented technologies for the Brazilian government. He led data and digital transformation at De Beers from 1994, then globally in 2001. In 2007, he joined Rio Tinto in London to lead global IT and Data. Moving to Singapore in 2015, he developed data strategies, later leading digital transformation at Alcoa in 2019. In 2022, he became Fonterra's Chief Data Officer. Throughout, he's leveraged AI to drive business value and is passionate about generative AI.

speakers.name: Stephanie Lopez
speakers.title: Chief of Staff to the CIO - Fonterra
speakers.bio: Steffie is the Chief of Staff for IT at Fonterra, supporting the digital transformation of 16.6 billion liters of milk annually into 30% of the worldâs dairy exports. With a background as an internationally experienced Senior Technical Business Analyst and seven years in technology transformation consulting, she won the PMO of the Year at the Irish PMI National Project Awards in 2019. Steffie collaborates closely with executives and partners to deliver strategic outcomes across technology and is a champion of diversity, guiding new talent in the corporate world.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

events.title: AI for the Environment Hackathon Festival 2024

coverImage.alt: Landing page of the AI Hackathon

events.shortDescription: AI Forum is hosting the 2024 AI Hackathon across Aotearoa and the South Pacific.

detailPageData.title: AI for the Environment Hackathon Festival 2024
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by SheSharp & AcademyEX

location.address: AcademyEX, 99 Khyber Pass Road, Grafton, Aucklandâ

fullDescription: AI Forum is hosting the 2024 AI Hackathon across Aotearoa and the South Pacific.

âWho can join?Anyone who wants to solve real-world problems in protect our water resources, fighting climate change, reduce pollution, manage land use, preserve biodiversity and improve the ways we experience our own environments.

Each hackathon team will pitch to a judging panel and regional winners will be announced. AIForum national judging panel will then select finalists to pitch their idea at the 2024 Aotearoa AI Summit, 11 September in Auckland.

This year, the AI Hackathon festival will be expanding on the problems to solve beyond the five key environmental outcomes (AI for the Environment Report 2022) to include Kaitiakitanga, embracing our connection to the land and surrounding natural environment fulfilling our roles as custodians.
The problems to solve are based on the six key environmental outcomes (AI for the Environment Report 2022). They have expanded these to make them more relevant to all participants in 2024.

Nicholas Fourie has been the Vice President of Information & Communication Technology at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare since February 2017. Since joining the company in 2007, he has held various systems engineering and management roles. Before his tenure at Fisher & Paykel, Nicholas worked for the South African division of BHP Billiton. He holds a Diploma in Computer Engineering from Damelin School of Information Technology in South Africa.

speakers.name: Nicholas Fourie
speakers.title: Vice President of ICT - Fisher & Paykel Healthcare
speakers.bio: Nicholas Fourie has been the Vice President of Information & Communication Technology at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare since February 2017. Since joining the company in 2007, he has held various systems engineering and management roles. Before his tenure at Fisher & Paykel, Nicholas worked for the South African division of BHP Billiton. He holds a Diploma in Computer Engineering from Damelin School of Information Technology in South Africa.Nicholas has also served on the Computer and Mathematical Sciences Industry Advisory Board for Auckland University of Technology (AUT). Regularly featured in the CIO50 New Zealand Top Award Winners, Nicholas achieved the number one ranking and won the prestigious award in 2023.â

speakers.name: Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Founder and Chair - She Sharp
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is the founder and the director of She Sharp , an Associate Professor in the School of Computer, Engineering and Mathematical Sciences at Auckland University of Technology and Chair of the AI Forum New Zealand. An internationally recognised leader in AI and machine learning, Dr. McCauley also serves on the boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and the AI Research Association, where she contributes to shaping the future of AI policy and research in New Zealand. Internationally, she has engaged as a Fulbright Scholar at North Carolina A&T State University, applying AI to agriculture and cybersecurity challenges.â

speakers.name: Rik Irons-Mclean
speakers.title: CTO, Enterprise Commercial - Microsoft AU & NZ
speakers.bio: As CTO, Rik drives outcomes through strategic data and digital tech adoption. Previously, he led Sustainability Sales Enablement at Microsoft and UK Strategy for manufacturing, energy, and resources. With 13 years at Cisco, Rik held global roles in energy, IoT, security, and digital transformation, including Chief Architect (CTO) for all industries. A leader in global conferences, Rik authors books, contributes to industry papers, and serves on boards for energy, manufacturing, sustainability, IoT, and digital skills. He's part of Australia's Climate Leaders Coalition and Sustainability Tech Council, holding an MBA in international leadership and MIEMA and CEnv sustainability accreditations.â

speakers.name: Justin Wood
speakers.title: Director - Deloitte New Zealand
speakers.bio: Justin has worked in technology for most of his career and has a long history working in Integration and specifically APIs across a range of industries but mostly in the financial sector. He has designed and implemented applications on public Cloud since the early days when there were only a handful of services available from Amazon Web Servicesâ

speakers.name: Yvonne Chan
speakers.title: Associate Dean | Board Director - AUT
speakers.bio: Meet Yvonne â a passionate advocate for authentic collaboration and transdisciplinary innovation driving positive social impact. As the Associate Dean and Director of Engagement for the Faculty of Design and Creative Technologies at AUT University, she fosters connections between academia, industry, government, and the community. With a background in Smart and high-performance textiles, Yvonne's expertise spans textiles, fashion, color, culture, and technology. Her work embodies the spirit of preserving traditional practices and embracing unorthodox materials and technology.â

speakers.name: Alex Mercer
speakers.title: Group Head of Marketing and Communications-Datacom
speakers.bio: Alex Mercer has worked across the Asia Pacific region and the U.S. for organizations such as Microsoft, Xero, Orion Health, NEXT Foundation, the Giving Pledge, Datacom, and Delightful Communications. Her career highlights include collaborating with talented leaders and achieving significant milestones.Alex is passionate about tech and enjoys navigating and leading through change using her brand marketing and communications skills, often handling crisis issues. She is also dedicated to New Zealand's social enterprise and business growth, actively investing in entrepreneurial businesses and helping to establish ArcAngels, an organisation that supports women-founded businesses.â

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Anne Newmarch
speakers.title: Consulting Analyst - Deloitte
speakers.bio: Anne works on the Deloitte AI and Data offering in the Generative AI Delivery team. She has hands-on experience implementing Generative AI solutions, such as Retrieval Augmented Generation Q+A and Text-to-insights over SQL Databases. Her technical experience is augmented by her traditional Computer Science background and her passion for delivering new and innovative solutions.

speakers.name: Blair Woods
speakers.title: CTO - Supahuman AI
speakers.bio: Currently CTO, and one of the three founders of New Zealand-based SupaHuman AI, Blair Woods started his professional life as a software developer where he progressed into various technical and people leadership roles. Throughout his professional life, Blair has worked in a range of organisations and industries: from start-ups through to international enterprise companies, finance and crypto through to AI, insurance, and utilities. He has also worked in the UK, South Africa, New Zealand, and Germany. Blair has a passion for AI with a focus on helping Australasian organisations to implement AI solutions so that they can improve efficiencies, offer new products and services, and ultimately expose new opportunities.

speakers.name: Chanelle Butters
speakers.title: Brand & Experience Designer - Datacom
speakers.bio: With a creative background spanning advertising, marketing, and film, Chanelle blends storytelling, art direction, and experience design to create compelling brands and engaging user experiences. At Datacom, she enjoys simplifying complex narratives and designing human-centred solutions that leverage emerging technologies to spark innovation in the future of work, education, and design. Chanelle is dedicated to making the world a more accessible place, particularly for neurodiverse and disabled communities.

speakers.name: Chase Bloch
speakers.title: Data Analyst - Fisher & Paykel Health Care, ICT
speakers.bio: Chase received his PhD in Political Science & Social Data Analytics from Pennsylvania State University in 2023. Since then, he has moved to New Zealand and joined the ICT Data Analytics team at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, where he works on report development and training to help the business make the most of their data.Chaseâs research focused on natural language processing and machine/deep learning, and he has used this experience to help Fisher & Paykel Healthcare start developing AI tools to gain deeper insights from their data.

speakers.name: Cherry Hsu
speakers.title: Learning & Development Advisor - F&PH
speakers.bio: Cherry is a Learning and Development Advisor at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare. She designs and develops eLearning and digital learning solutions to support learners. Cherry holds degrees in psychology and information systems from The University of Auckland. She has a great interest in exploring and optimising the use of learning technologies and AI in the learning and development space.

speakers.name: Chris Campbell
speakers.title: User Experience Manager-Fisher & Paykel Healthcare
speakers.bio: As a User Experience Designer in the medical device industry, I have a diverse background in experience design and Â interaction design. My expertise lies in System Thinking, User Experience, Device User Interface Design, and Human-Computer Interaction . Throughout my career, Iâve successfully led user-centric projects and guided creative teams to develop a diverse array of products. More recently I have been exploring the opportunities AI can offers in within the healthcare sector.

speakers.name: Dipesh Trikam
speakers.title: Emerging Product Lead - Datacom
speakers.bio: Dipesh isÂ a Â passionate technology evangelist who Â loves Â working Â at Â the Â intersection Â of Â business Â challenge, Â customer Â impact and technology, specifically how it can help create solutions which help organisations transform. Â Currently, he worksÂ as an Â Emerging Product Lead at Datacom.

speakers.name: Farhan Sattar
speakers.title: IT Technical Trainer - Microsoft
speakers.bio: With over 25 years in the IT industry, Farhan is a seasoned Business Program Manager and Learning & Development Technical Trainer at Microsoft. He blends deep technical expertise with a passion for growth. His background spans technical consulting, program management, and strategic development, with successful projects across sectors like NHS and Auckland Council.Holding multiple industry certifications, he's committed to staying ahead of tech trends, empowering through collaborative program development. Renowned for organizational agility and translating complexities into strategic plans, Farhan inspires a culture of learning.â

speakers.name: George Stevens
speakers.title: Technical Specialist - Microsoft
speakers.bio: George is a seasoned Administrator, Solutions Architect, and DevOps professional with a passion for problem-solving and teamwork. He thrives in Linux and Windows environments, excels in Azure and OpenAI technologies, and enjoys expanding his expertise in processes, networking, cloud, and Linux. Beyond technical skills, George is interested in optimizing how people use networks and systems daily, aiming to bring his supportive team experiences to new environments.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. speakers.name: Harpreet Singh
speakers.title: Software Engineer - Fisher & Paykel Healthcare
speakers.bio: I am a dedicated software engineer passionate about creating innovative solutions using cutting-edge technologies. I hold a degree in software engineering from Manukau Institute of Technology and have accumulated diverse experience in the industry.Currently, I work at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare as a software engineer, and previously, I have worked with Harvey Norman as a full stack developer. My expertise includes developing software, websites, and mobile apps, with a deep understanding of various programming languages and frameworks such as React, JS TypeScript, .NET Core, SQL, OOP, design patterns, Auth0, and Azure DevOps.

speakers.name: Jesse Luong
speakers.title: Business Analyst - Fisher & Paykel Health Care
speakers.bio: Combine finance, data and technology, I have experience in system implementation and process automation using AI and different data language. As a Business Analyst at F&P Healthcare, I analyse and develop data metrics, evaluate and optimize processes to enable business profitability. Iâm working to improve the efficiency of the company process in managing and reporting big and complex data environment.

speakers.name: Jeff Yeh
speakers.title: Marketing Specialist - Fisher & Paykel Healthcare
speakers.bio: Jeff is a digital marketing specialist with expertise in content management systems and web content. He utilizes AI for creative problem-solving to enhance everyday tasks and optimize digital marketing strategies.

speakers.name: Joe Thornley
speakers.title: Associate Director of Insight & Analytics- Datacom
speakers.bio: Joseph is a scientist at heart with a strong acumen for experimentation, quantitative analytics, and structured problem-solving. With over 20 years of experience in strategic direction, people leadership, and financial performance across strategy, sales, operation planning, digital transformation, and marketing, he excels in translating complex data into actionable business insights. Currently leading Datacom's AIOps and AI initiatives, he is at the forefront of applying generative AI to enhance IT operations and service delivery. His career is defined by his ability to lead complex initiatives from ideation to adoption, navigating and overcoming the intricacies of complex organizations.â

speakers.name: Kim Thibault
speakers.title: Chief Product Officer - BoardPro Limited
speakers.bio: Kim is an entrepreneur and product leader committed to creating ethical and impactful businesses. As a co-founder of BoardPro and other startups, including a faith-based humanitarian initiative, Kim brings experience establishing ownership structures, forming resilient founding teams, and the challenges founders face with bootstrapping and/or raising capital. Holding an MBA from the University of Auckland, Kim is driven by the transformative potential of business to positively impact communities and stakeholders.

speakers.name: Midu Chandra
speakers.title: General Manager for Futures & Insights - Datacom
speakers.bio: Midu has been working within the strategy, product, technology, marketing, and digital industries across Australia and New Zealand for over 25 years. His roles have focused on building high-growth and sustainable businesses, bringing a good balance of startup and corporate experience.

speakers.name: Monika Tylova
speakers.title: Mentor | Partnership Manager - She Sharp
speakers.bio: Monika's professional journey spans two continents: Europe and New Zealand. From piloting professional drones, she is now exploring the challenges present within the manufacturing industry. Drawing from her migrant experience, she underscores the significance of continuous self-development to succeed in diverse environments. With a keen interest in technology and AI, Monika is captivated by the relationship between humans and machines. Beyond her work, she's committed to helping others and champions the empowerment of communities through knowledge. She believes education is the key to brighter futures and is an advocate for more women taking the lead in digital transformation. Personally, she is also into fitness and motorbikes.

speakers.name: Tara Davidson
speakers.title: Category Marketing Manager - F&P Healthcare
speakers.bio: As a Category Marketing Manager at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Tara Davidson's fields of expertise include UX Design, Technology Implementation, Marketing Technology, Training, and Strategy. She leverages technological advancements to drive growth and enhance user experiences.

speakers.name: Troy May
speakers.title: Coach - Consulting
speakers.bio: Troy is an experienced business person and sharp observer, skilled at exploring different cultures and spotting cool trends. Great at breaking down complex ideas and spotting opportunities, knows how to turn experiences and observations into useful insights for projects and businesses, makes learning about business interesting and accessible.

speakers.name: Upeka De Silva
speakers.title: PhD student - Auckland University of Technology
speakers.bio: Upeka is currently a second-year PhD student at AUT, specializing in computer and information sciences with a focus on health informatics. Her research centers on speech analysis. Previously, she worked as a telecommunications engineer before transitioning to a career as a data scientist. Upeka also has some experience in academia. Her passion lies in applying her diverse background and skills to solve problems using AI technologies.

speakers.name: Zeeshan Bhatti
speakers.title: Head of Product Growth - BoardPro Limited
speakers.bio: Zeeshan Bhatti, a two-time entrepreneur and innovator with over 15 years in startups, SaaS, and AI-driven platforms, holds a PhD in Electrical Engineering from The University of Auckland. His strong academic background and practical expertise provide a significant advantage in deep-tech and early-stage startups. Specializing in SaaS and product-led growth, Zeeshan has hands-on skills in full-stack development, data science, data engineering, and AI/ML. His innovative strategies have driven growth and efficiency in various projects. In his spare time, Zeeshan enjoys exploring new technologies, reading about AI advancements, listening to podcasts, bird keeping, and gardening.

speakers.name: Ming Cheuk
speakers.title: Chief Technical Officer - AI Forum NZ
speakers.bio: Ming brings over a decade of experience in data and AI, spanning both academia and industry. As a co-founder of ElementX, he has spearheaded various AI projects across multiple sectors, including education, finance, manufacturing, defense, and retail. Passionate about demystifying complex AI concepts, he actively shares practical tips and insights at conferences and upskills organisations through workshops.

speakers.name: Maria Shcherbakova
speakers.title: Software Development Manager
speakers.bio: Throughout my eleven-year tenure at Fiserv, I found the most fulfilment in moments where conventional processes fell short of meeting business demands, necessitating creative thinking and bold leadership to instigate change. I consider myself fortunate to have had exceptional mentors early in my career and continuous opportunities for personal and professional development through confronting challenges head-on. Additionally, I prioritise transparency by openly acknowledging my strengths and areas of expertise. Recognising the significance of interpersonal dynamics and cultivating relationships to drive collective success is paramount in my approach to achieving business objectives.

speakers.name: Kiranjeet Kaur
speakers.title: Software Development Team Lead
speakers.bio: Kiran is a proactive .NET developer with 14 years experience in the analysis/ development/testing/deployment of Windows and web based applications using C#, ASP.Net, .NetCore, Azure and WPF, creating rich UI experiences along with worldwide client focused support, with demonstrable success multitasking under pressure to tight deadlines in the development team as well as individually, also developed extensive background in full software development life cycle. This includes requirement, gathering, design, coding, testing, debugging and maintenance.

speakers.name: Jazmin Vagha
speakers.title: Software Developer & Scrum Master
speakers.bio: Jazmin is a scrum master and intermediate software developer who is also an active member of the Fiserv Women tech organising committee, where she helps empower other women in tech! She worked with Fiserv during her AUT R&D project Consequently joined Fiservâs internship program after graduation then, secured herself a role as a Junior Software Developer

Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

speakers.name: Micayla Wright
speakers.title: Technical Lead, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: After studying Biological Sciences at university, Micayla had never dreamed of pursuing a career in software but was presented with an opportunity in the tech industry. From IT helpdesk to Tech Lead, Micayla's career in software engineering has spanned IT management, DevOps engineering, and technical leadership. She finds herself eight years later, evolved from supporting users to currently leading a feature team to drive technical vision and solutions.

speakers.name: Liz Miller
speakers.title: Director Digital Product, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: Before getting into product, Liz has had a varied career across digital marketing, events management, project management, digital production and eCommerce. Originally from Auckland, she has worked in London and Melbourne, and enjoys the variety that comes with working for a global company. Liz graduated with a BA/BCom in Communications and Marketing from Otago University with no plans to work in digital or tech, but is still loving it after more than 15 years in the industry.â

speakers.name: Effy Collis
speakers.title: Senior Product Manager, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: Effy likes to describe working in digital product as âadvanced cat herdingâ! While she holds degrees in mechanical engineering and psychology, Effy has chosen to flex her career into a variety of different spaces in the tech industry; from web dev to account management, UX design to team leadership. She prides herself on being a jack of all trades, and on finding creative yet simple solutions to the right problems.

speakers.name: Macy Ma
speakers.title: Senior Software Engineer, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: Macy has been working in the industry for 9 years, from university to her current job. Her career path has been quite straightforward, including working in front-end and full-stack engineering, but she also loves to do project management work, and help in product delivery. Her advice is to be a tech person, she always thinks tech is used to serve the business. There are no âfemaleâ developers, we are all developers!

speakers.name: Rebecca Fox
speakers.title: Salesforce Platform Manager, Les Mills Int'l
speakers.bio: Rebecca's career has stumbled around the world in various industries and roles, consistently gravitated around CRM & Salesforce. Starting with a degree in Computing and Management she has been using 'some' of these skills to bridge the gap between business process and systems since. Originally from the UK but now calling Auckland home she has worked globally in roles such as Data Analytics, Functional Consultant, Deployment Lead, QA and Project Management, before landing in a combination of them all with her current Platform role at LMI!â

speakers.name: Jess Zhang
speakers.title: Senior Data Engineer, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: Jess currently has six years of professional working experience. She started off as a software engineer before venturing into data engineering from a Computer Science and Information Systems background.Jess has worked in various industries namely Orion Health which is a digital healthcare company and Trade Me, New Zealandâs largest e-commerce site. The data landscape has never been more interesting and Jess is loving her current role to rebuild Les Millsâ data platform.â

speakers.name: Ramya Kommareddy
speakers.title: Delivery Lead, Les Mills International
speakers.bio: Ramya brings over 11 years of extensive experience in Quality Assurance and Test Management, characterized by a customer-centric approach. Her career journey has followed a clear path within the technology sector, starting in India before relocating to New Zealand seven years ago. Initially drawn by a desire for travel, however Ramya found herself deeply connected to Auckland, now considering it her home.Over the years, she has developed a passion for product development and delivery, leading in her recent transition to the role of Delivery Lead.

speakers.name: Annabelle Yoon
speakers.title: Senior Design Researcher, MYOB
speakers.bio: Annabelle is a qualitative Design Researcher with experience leading research to launch new products and services and improve existing ones. She distils complex findings into simple actionable insights and is a champion of the user. With a background in art and design, Annabelle enjoys impacting the design process through research, and collaborating with others to design amazing customer experiences. Annabelleâs experience includes working at agencies in Toronto and Sydney and for Yahoo! and Westpac Australia. Annabelle now lives and works in her hometown in Auckland.

speakers.name: Hazel Halil Halim
speakers.title: Senior Service Designer, MYOB
speakers.bio: Born in Bulgaria and raised in Turkey, Hazel pursued design studies in both Turkey and Italy, gaining insights into contrasts, shifting context. Having lived and worked in many countries, Hazel possesses a keen ability to navigate diverse environments. Initially arriving in Aotearoa for a six-month mission, she now finds herself almost five years later, deeply rooted in the kiwi culture. With a background in Service and Systems design, some of Hazelâs work includes mentoring start-ups in the social innovation sector in Paris, helping NGOs in Cambodia, and setting up an Innovation Center for SAP in Auckland.

speakers.title: Chief Analytics Officer, WWNZ
speakers.bio: âThe power of data is what links her multifaceted career steps together, starting as a strategy consultant and moving into in-house roles in different industries where growth was tied to data insights, loyalty and digital customer experience.âKathryn is currently the Chief Analytics Officer for Woolworths NZ, helping one of NZ's biggest businesses on a data-led transformation.âKathryn is in awe of the New Zealand data talent and believes that bringing different skillsets together will drive positive outcomes for individuals, teams, business and end customers.

events.title: Google Educator Conference 2023
events.shortDescription: The Google Educator Conference is designed for digital technology teachersâtraining them in STEM subjects and creative ways to present these in New Zealand classrooms.

detailPageData.title: Google Educator Conference 2023
detailPageData.subtitle: Google Educator Conference 2023
fullDescription: The Google Educator Conference is designed for digital technology teachersâtraining them in STEM subjects and creative ways to present these in New Zealand classrooms.

The purpose of the conference is to upskill all our valuable educators such as primary, intermediate, high school by presenting ideas to engage students with new technologies so they can be better equipped for the needs of twenty-first century learning.

Teachers will be equipped with the ability to tap into the creativity and enthusiasm that many students have for technology. And theyâll be exposed to exciting ways of presenting these technologies and master new skills that can directly shape the future of computer science in their classrooms.

speakers.name: Lesieli Oliver
speakers.title: ImmerseHer "Virtual Reality" Workshop Lead
speakers.bio: Lesieli, a proud Tongan, founder and Chief visionary officer of LÄlanga, LÄlanga dedicated to championing Maori-Pasifika student success. It empowers educators, school leaders with innovative mentoring programs, products like the LÄlanga ToolBox, and tech solutions.Lesieli recently completed her Masters Research, focusing on digital solutions to improve the success of Maori-Pasifika students, addressing issues such as truancy, wellbeing, and academic challenges. LÄlanga ToolBox, currently being used by 2,000 primary students in Auckland decile one schools. She received the University of Auckland 2023, 40 under 40 Alumni Inspiring leaders award and has launched a charity to empower Pasifika students and families through education.

speakers.name: Mehwish Hasan
speakers.title: Education Ambassador, QuiverVision
speakers.bio: Mehwish Hasan, a devoted educator, and the Computer Science Curriculum Leader at ACG Sunderland in Auckland is on a mission to bring excitement to the students' learning experiences. With a background in telecommunications engineering, Mehwish's unique perspective has led her to become New Zealand's inaugural QuiverVision Ambassador. This distinctive role empowers her to weave technology into education truly remarkably. Through the Quiver App, students can transform static, coloured images into dynamic, interactive marvels through augmented reality. This ingenious approach sparks creativity and guarantees that learning remains engaging and enjoyable.

speakers.name: Nils Reardon
speakers.title: Introduction to Augment Reality Workshop Lead
speakers.bio: Nils has been the CEO of QuiverVision since 2014, who brings a wealth of experience with a background in commerce and law. With an LLB (first class hons.) and a Commerce Degree (economics major), he honed his skills during six years at some of New Zealand's leading law firms before embarking on his journey with QuiverVision.Nils is the driving force behind the company's success and sets the course for its strategic direction. He also oversees the domains of finance and business management, ensuring the organisation operates at its peak potential. Nils is excited to see schools adopting technology that enhances the learning experience for both students and educators alike.â

speakers.name: Nischay Gupta
speakers.title: Introduction to Augmented Reality Workshop Lead
speakers.bio: Nischay is an experienced consultant in the tech sector. He has played a pivotal role in optimising operations & aligning strategies with QuiverVision's goals & objectives.Nischay led the launch of a new interactive EdTech product at QuiverVision to enable engaging learning experience, benefitting over 35,000+ teachers & 100,000+ students worldwide within the first year of rollout. He spearheads the expansion of our educational & curriculum-aligned content at Quiver. He holds a Master's degree in Engineering (1st-class hons.) at the University of Auckland. He is a research intern at Empathic Computing Lab (Auckland Bioengineering Institute) working on a project involving Virtual Reality, Biofeedback & Empathy.

speakers.name: Steve Smith
speakers.title: Google for Education, NZ Program Manager
speakers.bio: Steve has been a High School Social Studies and Geography teacher for the last 16 years in New Zealand since returning from teaching in London for 2 years. Steve worked to bring Google for Education to his school and along with a colleague trained the staff in the use of Google for education. Over 8 years, Steve passionately promoted Google Tools through workshops across New Zealand. He's Level 1 and 2 Certified Educator & Trainer, attended the 2019 Sydney Innovator Academy, and is part of the Google Earth Education Experts Group. He is now at Google's Auckland office as NZ Lead for Google for Education, aiming to empower NZ educator communities, support partners, and bring more "Google goodness" to education in NZ.

speakers.name: Claire Wigley
speakers.bio: Claire is currently a teacher at the innovative Spotswood College in New Plymouth. With a focus on teaching 21st-century skills, Claire's background lies in textiles technology and her teaching experience spans from year 1 to year 10. Over the past 8 years, she has expanded her knowledge to encompass various areas of the Technology Curriculum, ranging from cooking to coding and laser cutting. As an Inquiry Specialist teacher, Claire integrates the Design Thinking process into her lessons. In 2023, she took on the role of council member at TENZ and made significant contributions to their recent conference.

speakers.name: Alex Ward
speakers.title: Head of Digital Technology Kerikeri High School
speakers.bio: Alex is a Teacher, Musician, and Programming Enthusiast, leads Kerikeri High School's Digital Tech department for 4 years. Now, he's taking his talents to Rototuna Junior High School in Waikato. Alex combines code and creativity to craft generative art and music, even exploring the synergy between Digital Tech and other subjects. With a Bachelors in Music and experience as a Lecturer and Programme Administrator at Massey and Victoria University, his global performances include Singapore, Korea, China, Indonesia, Australia, and Antarctica. Alex relishes the switch to Digital Tech, making technology a canvas for creativity.â

speakers.name: Christopher Mende
speakers.title: Customer Engineering, Google Cloud NZ
speakers.bio: Christopher is the engineering lead for Google Cloudâs digital native customers. Heâs closely involved with NZ businesses creating a pathway for their journey to Cloud and AI, his work being showcased across industries and the globe.Christopher is also a facilitator in the Google Innovation Labs team that brings concepts to life using a methodology heavily focused on customer empathy. His work here involves joining the best of Google tech with market shaping concepts to move the needle on an organization's innovation journey.

speakers.name: Jennifer Gottschalk
speakers.title: Digital Technologies Teacher, Massey High School
speakers.bio: Jennifer currently teaches Digital Technology at Massey High School where she pushes, prods, cajoles and otherwise encourages her students (from year 9 to year 13) to become effective programmers and designers. Â Over the years Jennifer has contributed resources for teaching web design, databases (php / sql) and programming to TKI. Â She also administers the Usability / Heuristics Fails (and Successes) Facebook group where teachers and their students are encouraged to post examples of good and bad usability.

speakers.name: Julie McMahon
speakers.title: Lead Educator Grok Academy
speakers.bio: Julie, with over 30 years in Digital Technologies education, passionately inspires students to pursue careers in the field. She co-developed New Zealand's Digital Technologies curriculum, excelling in standards writing, leading assessment panels, and resource creation. Julie supports teachers through workshops and leadership roles, including DTTA's President. She's a curriculum lecturer at the University of Otago's Masters of Education Programme and earned the Ali Chivers Teacher of Influence Award and TENZ award for Subject Leadership in Technology Education, solidifying her as a luminary in the field.â

speakers.name: Aaron Overington
speakers.title: Education Strategist at Cyclone Computers
speakers.bio: Aaron has over 30 years of IT experience in diverse markets such as market research, freight, aviation, special education and disability. The last six and and half years have been in education, as the IT and Digital Learning Manager for a global school and more recently as the education strategist at Cyclone Computers. In this role Aaron works with kura across the motu to maximise the use of technology in the classroom to improve learning outcomes for all learners. He is also constantly looking at ways to address digital equity challenges schools face.

speakers.name: Evo Leota-Tupou
speakers.title: Founder of Pacific Kids' Learning
speakers.bio: Evo Leota-Tupou, an entrepreneur, mother of five, founder of Pacific Kids' Learning in Åtara. Evo actively engages in the EdTech community, focusing on Digital Learning and STEAM. She serves Executive Council of EdTechNZ and a member of Tech Women NZ. Evo is a representative on the Pacific Advisory Panel for Auckland Council and contributes to the National AI and Algorithm Expert Advisory Group for Te Whatu Ora. She advocates for neurodiverse learners and children with disabilities.Before returning to Auckland, Evo was in South Korea, she witnessed educational inequities and technological advancements in classes. Her mission is to build positive community outcomes for children, and linking early education to future opportunities.

speakers.name: Malcolm Clarke
speakers.title: President, Digital Technologies Teachers Aotearoa
speakers.bio: Malcolm has had 23 years of experience teaching Computing and Digital Technologies, 15 of which has been within NZ. He is happy to be known for being an 'early adopter' of all things related to education. Malcolm's journey in Aotearoa started off as Head of Digital Technologies at Secondary level teaching up to NCEA Level 3. This then led to gaining a position as the Edtech Educator at The Mind Lab in Wellington. This is where he further developed his passion for tinkering and trying new ideas out with coding and electronic peripherals in DigiTech. Malcolm is currently working at KÅwhai Intermediate School in Auckland where he has been the Specialist Digital Technologies teacher for the last five years.

speakers.name: Shanon O'Connor
speakers.title: Founder and Director of TÅnui Collab
speakers.bio: Shanon O'Connor (NgÄti Porou, NgÄi Tahu) is the Founder and Director of TÅnui Collab, a kaupapa MÄori driven STEMM learning provider, creating Â opportunities for rangatahi in te TairÄwhiti to explore the diversity and intersection of Science, Technology, Engineering, Maths and MÄtauranga MÄori so that the potential of rangatahi MÄori to be STEMM leaders and innovators can be realised. Shanon is a founding board member of Te Matarau, the MÄori Tech Association and also is a Council Member for EdTech NZ.

speakers.name: Tracy Henderson
speakers.title: Project Manager at University of Canterbury - CSER
speakers.bio: Tracy Henderson is experienced at leading Digital Technologies in the primary to pre-NCEA area. She has led numerous successful âseedâ projects to refine and learn how best to support teachers and school leaders to be empowered to teach and lead Digital Technologies in their schools and Communities of Learning / KÄhui Ako. She volunteers as secretary of the Digital Technologies Teachers Aotearoa (DTTA) subject association where she enjoys facilitating and implementing plans that support all teachers in Aotearoa. Her role at UC Computer Science Education Research Group (the âDepartment of Fun Stuffâ) focuses on carving new pathways and opportunities to empower teachers.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

She flourish the most in a security role that works right across the business, consultatively bringing security "into the real world" for the average staff member. Sai is passionate in helping others and promoting a wider understanding of cybersecurity.

fullDescription: Whilst 2020 started off with a bang, the start of a new decade, it quickly turned into unprecedented times. Yes, weâve heard the word âunprecedentedâ a lot over the past few months, but the truth is, the world just wasnât prepared for what was quickly evolving to be the biggest pandemic of this generation. For us here, in little olâ New Zealand, Kiwis too, persevered, making sacrifices for the preservation of the lives of others. She Sharp, in recognising these difficult times, decided

Thanks to everyone who was able to join us during May â June for the She Sharp Storytellers Series. Over five weeks, She Sharp hosted a range of tech leaders, from the likes of Google and Microsoft. We had participants tuning in from all around Aotearoa and across APAC. We hope that you were able to gain inspiration, motivation, and some new connections through the platform during these challenging times.

speakers.name: Saba Samiei
speakers.bio: Sabaâs mission in life is to do her best in creating a better future for humanity and all that lives around us. She has quite a diverse experience in the tech industry from hands-on coding to leading multi-million dollar transformation projects.Saba developed an in-depth passion for artificial intelligence back in 2017 and has dedicated her life outside of work to this topic ever since. She recently submitted her master thesis which focused on understanding the why behind the warnings about the danger of AI continues to work on this topic in more depth.

speakers.name: Tash BettridgeÂ
speakers.title: NZNWS - Co-Founder
speakers.bio: Tash Bettridge (Te whÄnau-a-apanui, ngapuhi, pakeha) is an award-winning woman in security, having co-founded the New Zealand Network for Women in Security, she was recognised in the global SIA Women in Security Forum Power 100 in 2022, Women in Security Awards Aotearoa Indigenous Advocate, amongst numerous industry accolades for her work advocating for diversity in cybersecurity, as well as initiatives that empower MÄori and Pacific People.

speakers.name: Dr Lena Waizenegger
speakers.bio: Lena is a Lecturer in Business Information Systems and the founder of the Early Career Academics forum at AUT. After completing her degree in international business and economics in Austria, France and Germany, she completed her PhD in Austria and New Zealand. Her research focuses on conversational agents and the impact on the human workforce, Artificial Intelligence, digital disconnection and robotic process automation. Her work has been published in top journals such as the Journal of Travel Research, Internet Research and International Journal of Knowledge Management. She regularly speaks at international conferences and her research was featured in renowned outlets like Forbes, Psychology today and the Daily Mail.

speakers.name: Alisa Edwards
speakers.bio: Alisa is a Chilean-American (and almost Kiwi) who is passionate about the environment, diversity & inclusion, and tech for good. She is active in Aucklandâs Salesforce community where she runs the Women in Tech group, works as a Salesforce consultant at GravityLab, teaches the Admin course at Tech Futures Lab, and collects 600+ Trailhead badges as well as ten Salesforce certifications. Alisa loves hearing from inspiring people, learning new languages, exploring vegan restaurants, hiking NZ Great Walks, attending hackathons, minimising waste, and finding ways to do things thoughtfully and a bit unconventionally.

speakers.name: Paola Golledge
speakers.bio: With over 15 years professional experience, Paola has spent most of her career working for large technology companies. She started her career as a business analyst in Europe and then moved to Australia where she worked in CA, Oracle and subsequently joined Google in 2011. For the first 8 years Paola was tasked to help build the Google Cloud Business across Asia Pacific and Japan. Most recently she has shifted careers and is now part of the Site Reliability Engineering team helping make some of the most loved products available for our users.

detailPageData.title: SUPERHERO DAUGHTER DAY
detailPageData.subtitle: This event, held to celebrate International Womenâs Day 2017 promotes technology as a career path of choice for young females
fullDescription: SheSharp, OMGTech, Colab AUT and Tech Girl Movement collaborated for Superhero Daughter Day 2017

The event celebrated International Womenâs Day and took place in AUT. Years 1 to Year 7 girls were invited to participate in workshops that involved Coding, Robotics and Unmaking. We saw 140 girls from different backgrounds come through for an action-packed day ready to learn and get techy.
The attendants also got to hear from Mikayla Stokes, Bright Sparks winner, who shared her story about how she developed her interest in technology.

events.shortDescription: On a sunny Thursday morning during half term a group of female high school and university students headed out to the Fisher and Paykel Healthcare offices in East Tamaki. Weâd all come to find out more about this iconic Kiwi company founded in 1934 and their pioneering work create medical apparatus for healthcare.

detailPageData.title: SHE# @ FISHER & PAYKEL
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and Fisher & Paykel
fullDescription: On a sunny Thursday morning during half term a group of female high school and university students headed out to the Fisher and Paykel Healthcare offices in East Tamaki. Weâd all come to find out more about this iconic Kiwi company founded in 1934 and their pioneering work create medical apparatus for healthcare.

We kicked the session off with some ice breaker games so everyone felt comfortable and familiar with each other. The team at Fisher and Paykel Healthcare then introduced themselves and gave an overview of the company, its challenges and achievements. This lead seamlessly into the group activity whereby the students worked in teams to come up with a solution to attached a breathing ventilator to a premature baby. They created prototypes using and a box of equipment provided and a toy baby! At the

After a quick lunch and networking session with some of the female engineers at Fisher and Paykel Healthcare we then kicked off the afternoon session with a panel discussion. This was an insightful and inspiring session with a group of female engineers who came from different backgrounds and teams within the company. It was a great illustration of the variety of engineering roles and disciplines Fisher and Paykel Healthcare products used.
By the end of our visit all our students were excited and inspired to pursue their dreams as engineers! Big thank you to Mandy McAlwee and the team at Fisher and Paykel Healthcare for having us.

The event was sold out, with over 60 women attending from around Wellington, some even travelling in from other cities around New Zealand.In addition to secondary and tertiary students, there were also industry members from a range of companies including Xero, 920 Career Agents, Fonde, Cat

The event also included a panel of women from Xero who shared successes, challenges and experiences from their own journeys. fullDescription: Imposter Syndrome is a long talked about topic that many people in the tech industry suffer from (not just women, either!). detailPageData.title: She Sharp Anita Borg Celebration
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp & Google
fullDescription: The Google Anita Borg Scholarship Alumni and SheSharp together hosted the Anita Borg birthday celebration to commemorate the truly inspiring woman she was. This was an event that happened globally in honour of Anita. We shared her incredible journey, stories and how she helped form a path for women to enter the computer world. She had such a strong passion for encouraging women into technology and continues to be an inspiration for women today.

The event had lots of cake, pizza, goodies and surprises! Teams created a short video about their passion for technology and thoughts on Anita Borg.Our guest speaker was illustrator and game developer Lianna Booton who talked to us about her journey into game development.

detailPageData.title: She Sharp @ Microsoft
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and Microsoft
fullDescription: This event brought speakers from various parts of the tech industry together for an evening of demos, lightning talks, networking and cupcakes. We were even lucky enough to get to try out the first ever Microsoft HoloLens in New Zealand!

Our speakers included Federica from Orion Health, Lance from Trade Me, Ella from Spark Ventures and Donna and Rocky from Microsoft. Attendees got to hear about analytics, the cute smart bin, Microsoft HoloLens and precision medicine.
Read more about our fantastic lightning speakers below!

speakers.name: Donna Wright
speakers.title: Business Strategy Director
speakers.bio: Donna, Microsoft NZ's Services Director since 2010, oversees Consulting and Support, driving Microsoft's Enterprise Customer empowerment. Returning in 2016 as Business Strategy Director, she concentrates on the Subsidiaryâs growth and transformation strategy. Previously, Donna dedicated 15 years to the Airline Industry in NZ and Australia, shaping IT strategy, leading e-commerce, digital transformation, and marketing solutions. Post her 2004 departure from Air NZ, Donna served as CIO at Coca-Cola Amatil NZ for 6 years. There, she facilitated mobile sales, automated warehousing, and implemented the global Coca-Cola Bottler SAP ERP solution. Donna holds a Bachelor's in Computer Science and an MBA from Auckland University.

speakers.name: Rocky Heckman
speakers.title: HoloLens Business Development Manager
speakers.bio: Rocky, pioneers holographic computing in the evolving era of technology. Microsoft's HoloLens stands as the worldâs initial fully untethered holographic computer, seamlessly merging the digital and physical domains. This innovation unlocks vast possibilities for Microsoft, its clientele, and collaborators. Driving this initiative aligns with his overarching goal of fostering the Digital Society.A regular presenter at industry events, including Microsoft Ignite, His expertise spans collaborations with governments, law enforcement, aviation, healthcare, finance, game studios, and ISVs. Prior to computer-centric career, he served as an aircraft mechanic, contributed to Cape Canaveral, and engaged with US government contractors.ââ

speakers.name: Lance Witheridge
speakers.title: Insights Analyst, TRADEME
speakers.bio: Originally from London, Lance unintentionally ventured into a tech career when a temp job turned permanent, evolving into a decade-long contracting position. As a software developer, he contributed to JP Morganâs Equities division in London and Tokyo, also working remotely from Christchurch. Later, he transitioned to a similar role in Westpacâs Financial Markets team in Auckland.Lance entered an Analytics role within Westpacâs mortgages team, eventually departing the world of banking and corporate attire for his current position as an Insights Analyst at Trade Me. Having been around long enough to attend a workshop on the nascent concept of "the internet," He is now applying that knowledge and exploring ways to capitalize on it.

speakers.name: Frederica Lanzo
speakers.title: Technical Business Analyst, Orion Health
speakers.bio: Federica is a Technical Business Analyst for the Data line of business, focused on modelling, storage, and retrieval of clinical data. She has been involved in strategic projects for North American and European customers, with the opportunity to widen her knowledge in insurance data.She joined Orion Health three years ago as a Graduate Software Test Engineer and since then, thanks to the improved technical skills acquired, she worked her way into a BA role.Federica has a degree in Biomedical Engineering with a 2 years specialization in Bioinformatics. Science and medicine are her passion, she could not be more excited to present Precision Medicine on behalf of Orion Health.

speakers.name: Ella Obreja
speakers.title: Head of Product, Lightbox
speakers.bio: Head of Product at Lightbox, which is part of Spark Ventures. In Ventures She worked on a variety of projects, prototyping new ideas, building a couple of apps for Spark and evangelising about APIs. Ella is a product geek who loves experimentation and Human Centred Design.

speakers.name: Michelle Sandford
speakers.title: Developer Engagement Lead Asia - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Tedx Speaker, developer community mentor, Microsoftie and international conference speaker. Michelle lives at the heart of the coding community and helps drive awareness and engagement as a technology evangelist. She advocates for STEM, the Games Industry and Developers. She presents at around 50 Conferences and Events around the world on Technology, Learning to Code, Open Source and AI. She judges hackathons and encourages the next generation into technology careers.

speakers.name: Renee Noble
speakers.title: Python Cloud Advocate - Microsoft
speakers.bio: Renee spends her time spread over a number of roles trying to improve the tech education offerings for people of all ages and levels of experience, particularly women and girls. She works as a Cloud Advocate at the Microsoft Reactor which allows her to connect with and share tech insight with the wider developer and student community, whilst running ConnectEd Code and school teaching takes her back to working with students and teachers, at the start of their tech journeys. Finally, the Girls' Programming Network gives her a focused opportunity to work with girls and women, spreading the program nationally, and to live the "if they can see it they can be it" motto.

speakers.name: Amir Mohammadi
speakers.title: Founder & Director - Promptech
speakers.bio: Dr. Amir Mohammadi founded Promptech.ai with a firm belief in the transformative power of AI, aiming to deliver comprehensive AI solutions tailored to enterprise needs. He developed AI teamspace to boost productivity, created prompt engineering toolkits, large language model libraries, and online courses. He is committed to lowering the access barrier in AI for businesses, researchers, and practitioners across Aotearoa.

speakers.name: Geri Harris
speakers.title: Senior Lecturer - AUT Business School
speakers.bio: Geri Harris is a senior lecturer at AUT Business School, integrating tech concepts into engaging discussions. A technology futurist, she seeks solutions to real-world problems across disciplines. With a PhD in Information Systems from UNSW Sydney, she has lectured for years and has 13+ years of consulting experience. Her philosophy inspires collaboration, knowledge sharing, and innovation, challenging the status quo. Geri's expertise in business, social, environmental, and ethical aspects of tech innovation helps hackathon participants explore the potential of emerging digital technologies in transforming business operations.

organizers.name: Sune Rogers
organizers.title: Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management - F&PH
organizers.bio: As the Head of ICT Planning & Portfolio Management at Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, Sune excels in mentoring, combining her analytical skills and creative flair to inspire her team. Her unique approach to problem-solving and strong belief in the power of intuition and communication make her an invaluable guide. Sune's dedication to personal and professional growth within her role showcases her as a transformative leader and mentor.

organizers.name: Melinda Legg
organizers.title: Ambassador | Event Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Mel currently works at ASB, as a digital experience analyst. She graduated at UoA in 2018 with a Bcom, but joining the tech industry happened to fall into her lap and she decided she loved working there! She has had experience as a business analyst in other corporate roles, as well as having the pleasure of working for non-profit organisations in the marketing space too. She has been nominated for many leadership programs in each company she has worked for, which is clearly proven in her strengths of leading with purpose, for cross-functional collaboration, between teams.She loves empowering other women who are also in the technology space, and loves being involved in creating a supportive, safe network for those that need it.â

organizers.name: Iuliia Shmykova
organizers.title: Ambassador | Data Insight Manager - She Sharp
organizers.bio: Iuliia is currently pursuing her Masterâs degree in IT Project Management at AUT, having shifted from Financial Analytics to the Product Development team at a bank. With the aim of enhancing the experience of making data-driven decisions in product management, she furthered her skills by obtaining a certificate in Data Analysis. She is inspired by the She# team and is thrilled to dedicate her time to connecting talented and enthusiastic people.

events.title: Security 101: Plugging the Data Leak
events.shortDescription: The awesome team at Flexware came and ran a security workshop with us. This involved learning about Ciphers, encrypting and decrpyting and working with Arduinos. The aim was to emphasise to everyone just how critical security is in addition to learning the basics of how it works. Check out the Flexware team below.

events.title: She Sharp - An Evening with Google
events.shortDescription: About 100 attendees joined SheSharp and Google team from Sydney for an evening at GridAKL. The first session addressed the issue of unconscious bias with Google sharing their training and knowledge around this area. This proved to be a great insight into how one of the biggest tech companies in the world is addressing the issue of diversity.

detailPageData.title: She Sharp - An Evening with Google
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and Google
fullDescription: About 100 attendees joined SheSharp and Google team from Sydney for an evening at GridAKL. The first session addressed the issue of unconscious bias with Google sharing their training and knowledge around this area. This proved to be a great insight into how one of the biggest tech companies in the world is addressing the issue of diversity.

After a short break of pizza and networking the google team jumped into the second session which was a more up close and personal. Each of the Googlers shared how they ended up working for Google, what their roles were, the office and culture.

During the evening students attending were highly encouraged to apply for Google internships and scholarships such as The Google Anita Borg Memorial Scholarship: Asia Pacific for university students and the Google Anita Borg Memorial Scholarship for First Years for high school students in their last year of study.

events.title: App Inventor Workshop @ Orion Health
events.shortDescription: Our first SheSharp event of 2016 was held at the Orion Health Head office in Auckland with over 40 female high school students from across Auckland attending.The morning kicked off with a team building challenge to break the ice and get the creative juices flowing.

detailPageData.title: App Inventor Workshop @ Orion Health
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and Orion Health
fullDescription: Our first SheSharp event of 2016 was held at the Orion Health Head office in Auckland with over 40 female high school students from across Auckland attending.The morning kicked off with a team building challenge to break the ice and get the creative juices flowing.

Throughout the workshop our SheSharp committee members and ambassadors help students develop and create a working app they could download and test on an android device. The workshop was aimed at all levels of students, from beginner to advanced, by the end of the workshop all the students had a working app.

events.title: Online Quiz Night celebrating Ada Lovelace Day
events.shortDescription: We have another amazing event coming up on the 15th October with our lovely She Sharp Alumni, Ruth James speaking at the Event! The event is to celebrate Ada Lovelace Day! In sponsorship with Xero!

detailPageData.title: Online Quiz Night celebrating Ada Lovelace Day
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and Xero
fullDescription: We have another amazing event coming up on the 15th October with our lovely She Sharp Alumni, Ruth James speaking at the Event! The event is to celebrate Ada Lovelace Day! In sponsorship with Xero!
Ada Lovelace Day was founded in 2009 by Suw Charman-Anderson to celebrate the achievements of women in science, technology, engineering and maths (STEM).
Our amazingly talented speaker, Ruth James is going to do the same by telling us about her journey in tech. She Sharp is honoured to have Ruth join us to celebrate this day!

speakers.company: Xero as their Tech Outreach and Engage Coordinator
speakers.bio: Ruth fell into the NZ tech industry by complete fluke when she arrived in the country thirteen years ago! Her first job was as a receptionist for a tech company (Fronde), since then sheâs worked for a number of New Zealand technology companies and currently works at Xero as their Tech Outreach and Engage Coordinator. She has thrown herself at every opportunity going where she can help enable, inspire and encourage school and university students to pursue a career in tech.

events.title: Women in AI for social good
events.shortDescription: From healthcare, to finance, education and government, artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping every part of our lives. It is also helping solve some of the worldâs greatest challenges. However, women account for less than 25 percent of the AI workforce.

detailPageData.title: Women in AI for social good
detailPageData.subtitle: Brought to you by She Sharp and AUT
fullDescription: From healthcare, to finance, education and government, artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping every part of our lives. It is also helping solve some of the worldâs greatest challenges. However, women account for less than 25 percent of the AI workforce.

Please join our next event to discuss women in AI for social good. Be inspired byDr Mahsa Mohagheghâsvision to increase female participation in AI, for the benefit of all. She will be joined by a panel of guest speakers to explore female-led AI thatâs good for the world. Join the conversation, network and create valuable connections.
